content.problemStatement:
There are NNN milky-dark chocolates. The ithi^{th}ith chocolate has a sweetness value of AiA_iAi​, and a bitterness value of BiB_iBi​. Note that both values can be negative.

You are going to eat all NNN chocolates in a period of KKK days.

On the first day, you start with the first chocolate. Each subsequent day, you continue eating from where you left off the previous day, consuming a contiguous block of chocolates.

Your mood for that day equals the **minimum** of total sweetness and total bitterness of the chocolates you ate.

Your overall mood equals the sum of moods during the KKK days. Find the **minimum** value of overall mood.

Note that, you must eat **at least** 111 chocolate each day and no chocolate should be left after KKK days.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of chocolates and the number of days, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the sweetness values of chocolates.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers, B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ — the bitterness values of chocolates.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** value of overall mood.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: You can eat 111 chocolate on the first day, 444 chocolates on the second day, and 222 chocolates on the third day to finish all NNN chocolates in KKK days.

Your mood for the first day is min⁡(A1,B1)=min⁡(−10,10)=−10\min(A_1, B_1) = \min(-10, 10) = -10min(A1​,B1​)=min(−10,10)=−10.
Your mood for the second day is min⁡(A2+A3+A4+A5,B2+B3+B4+B5)=min⁡(18,−12)=−12\min(A_2 + A_3 + A_4 + A_5, B_2 + B_3 + B_4 + B_5) = \min(18, -12) = -12min(A2​+A3​+A4​+A5​,B2​+B3​+B4​+B5​)=min(18,−12)=−12.
Your mood for the third day is min⁡(A6+A7,B6+B7)=min⁡(−16,−8)=−16\min(A_6 + A_7, B_6 + B_7) = \min(-16, -8) = -16min(A6​+A7​,B6​+B7​)=min(−16,−8)=−16.

Your overall mood is (−10)+(−12)+(−16)=−38(-10) + (-12) + (-16) = -38(−10)+(−12)+(−16)=−38, which is minimum possible.
Test case 222: The only possible way is to eat one chocolate each day.

Your mood for the first day is min⁡(A1,B1)=min⁡(10,12)=10\min(A_1, B_1) = \min(10, 12) = 10min(A1​,B1​)=min(10,12)=10
Your mood for the second day is min⁡(A2,B2)=min⁡(11,13)=11\min(A_2, B_2) = \min(11, 13) = 11min(A2​,B2​)=min(11,13)=11

Your overall mood is 10+11=2110+11=2110+11=21.
